name: Lafayette Neighborhood Housing Services, Inc Dba Homestead Resources
description_short: Non-profit housing services in Lafayette, IN offering affordable rental assistance and housing resources for low-to-moderate income residents.
description_long:
Lafayette Neighborhood Housing Services, Inc (operating as Homestead Resources) is a non-profit organization based in Lafayette, Indiana, with a mission to renew pride and confidence in Lafayette neighborhoods, promote reinvestment, and provide decent, affordable housing for low-to-moderate income persons. The organization appears primarily focused on connecting residents with low-income housing apartments and providing information about available rental programs in the Lafayette area.

The organization offers free informational resources to help prospective low-income housing applicants understand eligibility requirements, application processes, and available housing options. They provide guidance on income qualification thresholds (80% of area median income), documentation requirements, and eligibility criteria including citizenship status, Social Security numbers, and background screening standards. Their website serves as a directory of low-income housing apartments in Lafayette, including nearby properties like Fowler Apartments, Fifth Street Apartments, and Country View Estates.

What distinguishes Lafayette Neighborhood Housing Services is its embedded non-profit model providing free counseling and application support rather than acting as a property manager or direct landlord. They focus on neighborhood stabilization and community reinvestment while helping residents navigate the low-income housing landscape. The organization maintains a physical office location and phone line for direct contact, suggesting personalized assistance availability.

The primary limitation is that this appears to be a community resource organization with limited information publicly available about specific services beyond housing referrals. The website content is generic guidance rather than detailed program offerings. There is no evidence of specialized services like credit counseling, financial literacy programs, or emergency assistance beyond housing placement information. Potential applicants should contact them directly at (765)423-1284 to understand the full scope of personalized services available.
pros: Free information and resources—no fees for accessing housing guidance or application support
Non-profit mission focused on neighborhood reinvestment and community stability rather than profit extraction
Direct local presence with physical office (PO Box 252, Lafayette) and phone support at (765)423-1284
Provides clear eligibility guidelines aligned with HUD standards (80% AMI threshold)
Offers practical pre-application tips including documentation checklists and warnings about false information
Curates directory of nearby low-income housing options to help residents compare multiple properties
Appears to maintain current information (last modified June 2021) with zip code demographic data
cons: Limited public information about specific service offerings beyond housing referral and basic eligibility guidance
No evidence of specialized counseling services (credit repair, financial literacy, debt management) despite free-help category
Website relies on user Q&A section with no answers populated, suggesting limited active engagement or resource capacity
No information about waiting lists, application timelines, or organization capacity to serve clients
Unclear whether organization provides one-on-one counseling or primarily operates as an informational resource
best_for: Low-to-moderate income residents in Lafayette seeking affordable rental housing options
First-time low-income housing applicants needing guidance on eligibility and application processes
Individuals relocating to Lafayette who need local housing resource navigation
diagnosis: Best for low-income Lafayette residents seeking free guidance on finding and applying for affordable rental housing. Main caveat: the organization appears to function primarily as an informational resource and housing referral service rather than offering comprehensive financial counseling or crisis assistance—contact directly to confirm the scope of personalized services available.
services: Low-income housing apartment directory and referrals
Eligibility requirement guidance based on HUD standards
Application documentation checklists and preparation tips
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
